message:
feat(vdbe,core,pager): add index_ordered_scan_reliable flag to CodegenContext

The codegen previously assumed that index-based scans always produce
correctly sorted output, which is not true for MemDatabase backends
where indexes do not maintain key-sorted iteration order. This caused
incorrect ORDER BY results when the optimizer chose an index-assisted
scan path.

Changes:
- Add `index_ordered_scan_reliable: bool` field to CodegenContext with
  default false, so the conservative path (sorter fallback) is used
  unless explicitly opted in.
- Guard `resolve_order_by_index_plan` behind the new flag so codegen
  only attempts index-ordered scans when the backend guarantees it.
- Restrict index-seek SELECT to queries without ORDER BY, since the
  index seek returns rows in insertion order, not sort order.
- Update all CodegenContext construction sites in connection.rs to use
  `..CodegenContext::default()` for forward compatibility with new
  fields.
- Remove dead `project_join_column` function from connection.rs.
- Refactor pager cache lock acquisition to avoid holding the lock
  across the `if let Some` body, improving borrow clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
